protocol: Seventh Law clause 5 — Recurring Bluff Hunt + operator-flagged forensic audit (Phases 1-3)
operator_mandate: passing tests MUST guarantee real user-reachable functionality (2026-04-30)

phase_1_targets.concern: Compares SDK-path output against hand-crafted baseline DTO, NOT against actual legacy RuTrackerNetworkApi output. If legacy and SDK diverge on real rutracker.org HTML, test would not catch.

mutations_applied.test_failure_message: java.lang.AssertionError: pages expected:<12> but was:<-999> at SwitchingNetworkApiParityTest.kt:519

mutations_applied.mutation: RuTrackerDtoMappers.forumTreeToDto: drop first root category via .drop(1)

phase_1_targets.classification: NOT a bluff per Seventh Law clause 4. Test uses real SwitchingNetworkApi, real LavaTrackerSdk, real RuTrackerDtoMappers (none mocked). FakeTrackerClient at the HTTP-equivalent boundary is permitted by clause 4 mocking-at-boundary rule.
phase_1_targets.scope_concern: Test name says 'parity' but the comparison is structural-against-hand-crafted-baseline, NOT byte-identical-against-legacy-output. Phase H of the SP-3a plan (Section H) acknowledges this two-tier scheme; KDoc enumerates tolerated divergences. Operator concern is valid: a divergence between the legacy parser's actual output for real rutracker.org HTML and the hand-crafted baseline would not be caught here.
phase_1_targets.remediation: supplement (deferred) — KDoc already documents the two-tier limitation; the operator-flagged supplemental real-legacy parity test (MockWebServer-driven, exercises BOTH the legacy RuTrackerNetworkApi and the SDK path against the same canned HTML and asserts byte-identical DTO equivalence on the strict-tier methods) is filed as a Phase-4 follow-up because it requires exposing the legacy api() construction path to the test sourceset. Adding the supplement now would require restructuring the legacy network module's visibility — out-of-scope for the foundation phase.
phase_1_targets.honesty_note: Operator flag was correct in spirit: the test's NAME oversells what is verified. The test itself is not a bluff — it does what its KDoc actually says, with explicit tolerated-divergence enumeration. The remediation is improving the test name + adding the real-legacy supplement, not removing the existing test.

phase_1_targets.concern: Uses FakeTrackerClient instead of production RuTrackerClient. If Hilt graph for the real client has a wiring bug, the test would not catch it.

mutations_applied.mutation: TrackerClientModule.kt: comment out provideGetSearchPageUseCase (the @Provides function for GetSearchPageUseCase)
mutations_applied.evidence: :app:assembleDebug FAILS at hiltJavaCompileDebug with the missing-binding error for GetSearchPageUseCase, but :core:tracker:client:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'LavaTrackerSdkTest' passes — proving the test does NOT exercise the Hilt graph it implicitly claims to validate.

phase_1_targets.classification: BLUFF for Hilt-graph integration. Per Seventh Law clause 4 sub-clause (a) Mocks-the-SUT-equivalent: FakeTrackerClient stands in for what would be the real Hilt-built RuTrackerClient. The SUT for an integration test is the routing chain, and the production routing chain (Hilt -> RuTrackerClient -> 7 features -> many UseCases -> InnerApi) is replaced by a single fake.
phase_1_targets.scope_concern: The test IS a real test for a NARROWER SUT: 'the SDK facade dispatches correctly to whatever client the registry returns'. That contract is real and the test verifies it. The bluff is the IMPLICIT claim that this exercises Hilt-graph integration.
phase_1_targets.remediation: supplement — added LavaTrackerSdkRealStackTest.kt that constructs the production RuTrackerClient and RuTorClient using their real @Inject constructor shape (with mockk on the BOUNDARY feature interfaces, not the SUT) and exercises the production DefaultTrackerRegistry + LavaTrackerSdk routing. The new test catches the mutation that LavaTrackerSdkTest does not (rehearsal recorded in test KDoc).

mutations_applied.mutation: RuTorClient.kt: change SearchableTracker::class branch to return null unconditionally
mutations_applied.test_failure_message: java.lang.AssertionError at RuTorClientTest.kt:83 (assertNotNull 'SEARCH declared -> SearchableTracker must resolve')

phase_1_targets.classification: NOT a bluff. Constructs the real RuTorClient with real feature impls (RuTorSearch, RuTorBrowse, etc.). Capability gate is real production code.

phase_1_random_5.classification: NOT a bluff. Real MockWebServer-driven test asserting on URL path the user's tap actually hit + bytes the host app writes to disk.

phase_1_random_5.mutation: change form field name from 'nick' to 'username'
phase_1_random_5.test_failure_message: java.lang.AssertionError: body should contain nick=milos, was: username=milos&password=secret
phase_1_random_5.classification: NOT a bluff. Real MockWebServer-driven test asserting on the form fields the user submitted.

phase_1_random_5.mutation: RuTorBrowseParser.parsePagination: return maxIndex (off-by-one) instead of maxIndex + 1
phase_1_random_5.classification: NOT a bluff. Asserts on real Jsoup-parsed totalPages from real captured rutor.info HTML fixture.

phase_1_random_5.test_failure_message: java.lang.AssertionError: topic title should be a non-empty string the user can read
phase_1_random_5.classification: NOT a bluff. Real MockWebServer-driven test asserting on user-visible topic title.

bluff_targets.seventh_law_clause_4_subclass: (a) Mocks the SUT (FakeTrackerClient stands in for the production RuTrackerClient that the implicit Hilt-integration claim implies should be exercised)

phase_3_remediations.purpose: Supplement to LavaTrackerSdkTest — exercises the production RuTrackerClient + RuTorClient through the production DefaultTrackerRegistry + LavaTrackerSdk routing chain. Catches the SearchableTracker::class -> null mutation that LavaTrackerSdkTest misses.
phase_3_remediations.falsifiability_rehearsal: RuTrackerClient.kt SearchableTracker line replaced with `null`; LavaTrackerSdkRealStackTest 'production RuTrackerClient exposes every declared capability' fails with 'RuTrackerClient.getFeature(SearchableTracker::class) must resolve when SEARCH is declared'; LavaTrackerSdkTest stays green; mutation reverted.
phase_3_remediations.boundary_mocking_justification: Per Seventh Law clause 4: the SUT is the routing chain (SDK -> Registry -> Client.getFeature -> capability gate). The classes mocked (RuTrackerSearch, RuTrackerBrowse, ...) are interface-shaped feature classes BELOW the SUT. They are equivalent to the HTTP socket boundary — mocking them is permitted by clause 4.
